Label: Dataset
Description: A set of RDF triples published, maintained, or aggregated by a single provider and described using the VoID vocabulary.

Label: Concept Count Analysis Result
Description: A result of a concept count analysis.

Label: Dataset
Description: An item that refers to a collection of data from a data source.

Label: Disease
Description: A disorder of structure or function, especially one that produces specific signs, phenotypes, or symptoms, or affects a specific location and is not simply a direct result of physical injury.

Label: Disease To Phenotypic Feature Association
Description: An association between a disease and a phenotypic feature in which the phenotypic feature is associated with the disease in some way.

Label: Entity To Disease Association
Description: An association between an entity and a disease, represented as a typed Biolink association with subject, predicate, object, evidence, and provenance slots.

Label: Log Odds Analysis Result
Description: A result of a log odds ratio analysis.

Label: Phenotypic Feature
Description: A combination of entity and quality that makes up a phenotyping statement; an observable characteristic of an individual often resulting from genotype-environment interaction.

Label: Phenotypic Feature To Phenotypic Feature Association
Description: Association between two phenotypic feature concept nodes, qualified by the predicate used; often used for similar_to or member_of relationships.

Label: Study
Description: A detailed investigation and/or analysis.

Label: modified
Description: Date on which the resource was changed.

Label: last updated on
Description: Date on which the resource was last updated.

Label: version
Description: The version number or version identifier of a resource.

Label: distinct objects
Description: The total number of distinct objects occurring in a dataset or property partition.

Label: distinct subjects
Description: The total number of distinct subjects occurring in a dataset or property partition.

Label: properties
Description: The total number of distinct properties used in a dataset.

Label: property
Description: The RDF property that defines a VoID property partition.

Label: property partition
Description: Relates a dataset to a subset containing only triples with a specific predicate.

Label: triples
Description: The total number of triples contained in a dataset or linkset.

Label: label
Description: A human-readable name for a resource.

Label: category
Description: Name of the high-level ontology class in which an entity is categorized; in RDF this often corresponds to a Biolink class URI.

Label: concept count object
Description: The number of instances in a dataset or cohort whose records contain the concept in the object slot of an association.

Label: concept count subject
Description: The number of instances in a dataset or cohort whose records contain the concept in the subject slot of an association.

Label: concept pair count
Description: The number of instances in a dataset or cohort whose records contain both the subject and object concepts of an association.

Label: dataset count
Description: The total number of instances, such as patients or rows, in a dataset or cohort.

Label: has study results
Description: Connects a study to instances of its study result.

Label: has supporting studies
Description: Studies that produced information used as evidence to generate the knowledge expressed in an association.

Label: log odds ratio
Description: The natural logarithm of the odds ratio estimated for the association in a sample population.

Label: log odds ratio 95 ci
Description: The ninety-five percent confidence range in which the true log odds ratio for the sample population falls.

Label: object
Description: Connects an association to the object of the association; for example, in a gene-to-phenotype association, the phenotype is the object.

Label: predicate
Description: A high-level grouping for the relationship type; also known as the minimal predicate for an association.

Label: subject
Description: Connects an association to the subject of the association; for example, in a gene-to-phenotype association, the gene is the subject.

Label: supporting data set
Description: Connects an analysis result to the supporting dataset on which the result is based.

Label: total sample size
Description: The total number of samples or observations included in the analysis result.
